posts.text: We were about halfway through completing the Panama Canal in the amount of time it has taken California to finish its little bridge for woodland creatures

The Hoover Dam was built in the amount of time it has taken California to construct a small bridge for cats and butterflies. The Empire State Building was built in about a quarter of the time.


Christopher F. Rufo ⚔️ (@christopherrufo)

EXCLUSIVE: Gavin Newsom promised to build a bridge for cougars and butterflies in the middle of Los Angeles. The project has turned into another boondoggle, with broken deadlines and costs exploding to $114 million.

This is Newsom's bridge to nowhere.
city-jour

Yes. Thank God. We need to support and celebrate this judge. She’s one of the few with the courage to treat these scumbags exactly as they deserve to be treated. We don’t have to be held hostage by the worst among us. We can actually punish them.


Collin Rugg (@CollinRugg)

NEW: Family of a Texas teen loses it as judge sentences him to 25 years in prison for robbing a convenience store.

Judge Raquel West was seen torching 18-year-old Caden James Fontenette before handing out the sentence.

Hey I recognize this show




Collin Rugg (@CollinRugg)

Kouri Richins, who was just found guilty of killing her husband by poisoning his drink, once sat down for a 5-minute interview with a local show in Utah to promote her book.  

Richins appeared emotionless as she promoted the book.  

"My husband passed away unexpectedly last year... It completely took us all by shock..."

Video
  





— https://nitter.net/CollinRugg/status/2034021941380845705#m

Rebellion in our home. Some of the kids are claiming that I force them to watch this every year and it’s “boring.” Sounds like we need to increase the viewings to three times a year until morale improves.
Time for our annual St Patrick’s Day viewing of a cinematic masterpiece

There is no good moral reason why she should not simply be taken outside the morning after her conviction and hanged. This woman has lost her right to exist.

This is, of course, retarded. The great men of history were deeply introspective. They wrote memoirs, journals, diaries, poetry. They were philosophers, theologians. They yearned. They were romantics. They were in fact much more introspective than the average person today.
